note_data.title: Chemistry of our environment
keyPoints: Matter is made up of particles in three states: Solid (Fixed shape, High Density) - Particles close together, Orderly Arranged, Held by strong forces in fixed positions
Liquid (No fixed shape, High Density) - Particles close together, NOT in order, Strong forces, Vibrate & Free to move
Gas (No fixed shape, Low density) - Particles Far apart, Weak forces, Free to move around - Pressure is due to the particles bouncing off walls of container
Melting (Solid → Liquid) - Particles gain energy, Vibrate more, Occurs at melting point, Endothermic Reaction (Because particles gain energy to break bonds)
Freezing (Liquid → Solid) - Occurs at freezing point (Also the m.p. of pure substance), Exothermic Reaction (Because particles give out energy to form bonds)
For both Freezing & Melting, temp. remains constant during the process
Compound - 2 or more elements chemically joined together - Contains Covalent or Ionic Bonds
Mixtures - 2 or more substances which are not chemically joined together - Can consist of: Elements, Compounds, Elements + Compounds

definitions.definition: Heat or energy ENTER (absorbed/gained) to break bond
Heat, energy EXIT (give out/lose) to form bonds
No more solid can dissolve in it

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give practical solutions that could realistically be implemented
Give the process step by step in correct order

modelAnswer.question: Explain how sulfur dioxide in the atmosphere contributes to environmental problems. [3]
modelAnswer.answer: Sulfur dioxide dissolves in rainwater to form sulfurous acid. This makes the rain acidic with pH less than 7. Acid rain damages limestone buildings, kills fish in lakes and harms plant growth.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Sulfur dioxide dissolves in rainwater/forms acid
Mark 3: Any one harmful effect - damages buildings/kills fish/harms plants

commonMistakes: Confusing endothermic and exothermic reactions - remember EN means energy ENTER, EX means energy EXIT
Thinking temperature changes during melting and freezing - it remains constant during both processes
Confusing compounds and mixtures - compounds are chemically joined, mixtures are not chemically joined
examinerTips: Any question that wants the method to obtain (make) crystals, the process will be 'Crystallisation'
Use Electronic Balance to measure Mass of chemical, Thermometer for Temperature, Measuring Cylinder OR Burette OR Pipette for Volume of gas
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Carbon cycle showing photosynthesis removing CO₂, respiration and combustion releasing CO₂, with arrows indicating direction of carbon movement between atmosphere, plants, animals and fossil fuels
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to explain the environmental impact of sulfur dioxide emissions and describe methods to reduce acid rain
N 2022 P1 Q12 (1 mark) — multiple choice question on identifying the main greenhouse gas contributing to global warming
M/J 2022 P2 Q6(b) (4 marks) — students had to explain how catalytic converters reduce harmful emissions from car exhausts
N 2023 P2 Q5 (5 marks) — question on the carbon cycle and how human activities disrupt natural processes

workedExample.question: Explain how acid rain is formed and describe two methods that can be used to reduce its formation. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify the source - Sulfur dioxide (SO₂) is released when fossil fuels containing sulfur impurities are burned in power stations and factories
Step 2: Explain the atmospheric reaction - SO₂ reacts with oxygen and water in the atmosphere: SO₂ + ½O₂ + H₂O → H₂SO₄
Step 3: Describe acid rain formation - Sulfuric acid (H₂SO₄) falls as acid rain with pH below 5.6
Step 4: Method 1 - Remove sulfur from fossil fuels before burning (desulfurization)
Step 5: Method 2 - Use flue gas desulfurization (scrubbing) with calcium carbonate to remove SO₂ from waste gases
workedExample.answer: Acid rain forms when sulfur dioxide from burning fossil fuels reacts with oxygen and water in the atmosphere to produce sulfuric acid (SO₂ + ½O₂ + H₂O → H₂SO₄). Two methods to reduce formation: (1) Remove sulfur from fossil fuels before combustion, (2) Use flue gas desulfurization with limestone scrubbers to remove SO₂ from emissions.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for SO₂ source, 1 mark for atmospheric reaction, 1 mark for acid formation, 1 mark for each method (2 marks), 1 mark for explaining how methods work

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused ozone depletion with global warming' — always distinguish between UV protection (ozone layer) and heat retention (greenhouse effect)
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often failed to include balanced chemical equations when explaining acid rain formation' — always write SO₂ + ½O₂ + H₂O → H₂SO₄ for full marks

Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates frequently gave vague answers about reducing pollution' — be specific about methods like catalytic converters, desulfurization, and renewable energy rather than just saying 'use less fuel'
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw the carbon cycle showing: atmosphere (CO₂), photosynthesis (plants absorbing CO₂), respiration (organisms releasing CO₂), combustion (fossil fuels releasing CO₂), and carbon storage in fossil fuels, oceans, and biomass. Include arrows showing carbon movement between reservoirs and label human activities that increase atmospheric CO₂.
note_data.quickRecap: Matter consists of particles in three states with different arrangements and forces. Changes of state involve energy transfer - melting is endothermic, freezing is exothermic, both occur at constant temperature. Compounds are chemically joined elements while mixtures are not chemically joined substances.
